message:
refactor(web): remove 'use no memo' directives from hooks

Removes the React Compiler opt-out directive from hooks, as the updated
vite.config.ts now scopes the compiler to /src/components/ only. These
hooks are no longer processed by the compiler, making the directive
unnecessary.

Hooks affected:
- useCAAM.ts
- useFleet.ts
- usePipelines.ts
- useSafetyPosture.ts
- useSetup.ts
- useSnapshot.ts

The scoped compiler configuration in vite.config.ts provides better
control than per-file directives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"bd-1hnyy","title":"Cost Attribution Custom Tags","description":"## Overview

User-defined tags on agents/sessions that flow to cost records, enabling custom slicing of cost data (by feature, sprint, team, experiment).

## Background & Justification

Cost tracking already has dimensions:
- Organization
- Project
- Agent
- Task
- Session

But these are fixed. Teams need custom dimensions:
- Feature (which feature is this work for?)
- Sprint (which sprint to bill?)
- Team (which team owns this cost?)
- Experiment (A/B testing cost comparison)

Custom tags enable project-specific cost analysis without code changes.

## Key Components

### 1. Tag Data Model

```typescript
interface CostTag {
  key: string;    // e.g., "feature", "sprint", "team"
  value: string;  // e.g., "auth-v2", "sprint-42", "platform"
}

// Tags can be set at multiple levels
interface TagScope {
  scope: 'agent' | 'session' | 'task' | 'manual';
  agentId?: string;
  sessionId?: string;
  taskId?: string;
}
```

### 2. Tag Propagation

Tags flow to cost records:
- Agent-level tags → all costs for that agent
- Session-level tags → costs during that session
- Task-level tags → costs for specific task
- Manual tags → applied retroactively

Inheritance: Agent > Session > Task (more specific wins)

### 3. Tag Management

**Setting Tags:**
- UI: Tag editor on agent/session detail
- API: Set tags on creation or update
- CLI: `--tags feature:auth,sprint:42`

**Tag Validation:**
- Key: alphanumeric + underscore, max 50 chars
- Value: any string, max 100 chars
- Max 10 tags per scope

### 4. Cost Analytics Integration

Extend cost queries:
```sql
-- Costs by feature
SELECT tag_value, SUM(cost_millicents) 
FROM cost_records 
JOIN cost_tags ON ...
WHERE tag_key = 'feature'
GROUP BY tag_value;

-- Compare experiments
SELECT 
  tag_value AS experiment,
  SUM(cost_millicents) AS total_cost,
  COUNT(DISTINCT agent_id) AS agents
FROM cost_records
JOIN cost_tags ON ...
WHERE tag_key = 'experiment'
GROUP BY tag_value;
```

### 5. UI Components

**Tag Editor:**
- Key-value input fields
- Autocomplete for existing keys
- Add/remove tags

**Cost Filter by Tag:**
- Tag key dropdown
- Tag value multi-select
- "Group by tag" option

**Tag-based Reports:**
- Cost by tag key chart
- Tag value breakdown table
- Tag trend over time

### 6. API Extensions

- `POST /api/agents/:id/tags` - Set agent tags
- `GET /api/agents/:id/tags` - Get agent tags
- `POST /api/sessions/:id/tags` - Set session tags
- `GET /api/cost-analytics/by-tag` - Query costs by tag
- `GET /api/tags/keys` - List used tag keys
- `GET /api/tags/values/:key` - List values for key

## Database Schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE cost_tags (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  cost_record_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  tag_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  tag_value TEXT NOT NULL,
  source_scope TEXT NOT NULL, -- agent, session, task, manual
  source_id TEXT,
  created_at TEXT
);

CREATE INDEX idx_cost_tags_key ON cost_tags(tag_key);
CREATE INDEX idx_cost_tags_key_value ON cost_tags(tag_key, tag_value);

CREATE TABLE tag_definitions (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  description TEXT,
  allowed_values TEXT, -- JSON array, null = any
  created_at TEXT
);
```

## Implementation Tasks

1. Design tag data model and schema
2. Add tag tables to database
3. Extend agent/session APIs with tag endpoints
4. Implement tag propagation to cost records
5. Extend cost analytics queries for tag filtering
6. Build tag editor UI component
7. Add tag filters to cost analytics UI
8. Create tag-based report views
9. Implement tag autocomplete
10. Write unit tests for tag propagation
11. Write integration tests for cost queries
12. Write E2E test for tag flow

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tags can be set on agents, sessions, tasks
- [ ] Tags propagate to cost records automatically
- [ ] Cost analytics can filter/group by tags
- [ ] Tag autocomplete shows existing keys
- [ ] Reports break down costs by tag values
- [ ] Tag inheritance works correctly

## Dependencies

+{"id":"bd-2hv7t","title":"Pipeline Templates Library","description":"## Overview

Reusable pipeline patterns (CI/CD, multi-step refactor, test-then-commit) with import/export and team sharing. Reduces boilerplate for common multi-agent workflows.

## Background & Justification

The pipeline.service.ts supports complex workflows with:
- Multiple step types (script, agent, conditional, loop, parallel)
- Approval workflows
- Retry policies
- Checkpointing

But creating pipelines is manual and complex. Templates lower the barrier for common patterns.

## Key Components

### 1. Template Categories

**CI/CD Patterns:**
- Build → Test → Deploy
- PR Review Pipeline
- Release Checklist

**Refactoring Patterns:**
- Multi-file Refactor with Tests
- Dependency Update Flow
- Code Migration Pipeline

**Quality Patterns:**
- Test Coverage Improvement
- Documentation Sync
- Linting + Formatting

**Custom:**
- User-defined templates

### 2. Template Data Model

```typescript
interface PipelineTemplate {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  category: string;
  
  // Pipeline definition
  steps: PipelineStep[];
  variables: TemplateVariable[]; // User-configurable params
  
  // Metadata
  author?: string;
  version: string;
  tags: string[];
  usageCount: number;
  rating?: number;
}

interface TemplateVariable {
  name: string;
  type: 'string' | 'number' | 'boolean' | 'select';
  description: string;
  defaultValue?: any;
  options?: string[]; // For select type
  required: boolean;
}
```

### 3. Built-in Templates

**Test-Then-Commit:**
```yaml
variables:
  - name: testCommand
    type: string
    default: "bun test"
steps:
  - type: script
    name: Run Tests
    command: "{{testCommand}}"
  - type: conditional
    condition: "{{previousStep.exitCode}} == 0"
    onTrue:
      - type: script
        name: Commit Changes
        command: "git commit -am 'Changes verified by tests'"
    onFalse:
      - type: notification
        message: "Tests failed, skipping commit"
```

**Multi-File Refactor:**
```yaml
variables:
  - name: pattern
    type: string
    description: "File pattern to refactor"
steps:
  - type: parallel
    items: "{{glob(pattern)}}"
    step:
      type: agent
      config:
        template: refactoring-expert
        prompt: "Refactor {{item}}"
  - type: agent
    name: Verify All
    config:
      prompt: "Run full test suite and verify refactoring"
```

### 4. Variable Resolution

Templates use Mustache-style variables:
- `{{variableName}}` - Simple substitution
- `{{glob(pattern)}}` - File glob expansion
- `{{env.VAR}}` - Environment variable
- `{{previousStep.output}}` - Step chaining

### 5. UI Components

**Template Browser:**
- Category sidebar
- Template grid
- Search/filter
- Preview panel

**Template Configurator:**
- Variable form inputs
- Step visualization
- Dry-run preview
- "Create Pipeline" button

**Template Editor:**
- YAML/JSON editor
- Visual step builder
- Variable definition UI
- Validation feedback

### 6. API Endpoints

- `GET /api/pipeline-templates` - List templates
- `GET /api/pipeline-templates/:id` - Template detail
- `POST /api/pipeline-templates` - Create template
- `PUT /api/pipeline-templates/:id` - Update template
- `DELETE /api/pipeline-templates/:id` - Delete template
- `POST /api/pipeline-templates/:id/instantiate` - Create pipeline from template
- `POST /api/pipeline-templates/import` - Import template
- `GET /api/pipeline-templates/:id/export` - Export template

## Database Schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE pipeline_templates (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  category TEXT,
  definition TEXT NOT NULL, -- JSON
  variables TEXT, -- JSON array
  author TEXT,
  version TEXT,
  tags TEXT, -- JSON array
  usage_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  is_built_in INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  created_at TEXT,
  updated_at TEXT
);
```

## Implementation Tasks

1. Design template data model
2. Create `pipeline-template.service.ts`
3. Implement variable resolution engine
4. Create built-in template library
5. Add API routes
6. Build template browser UI
7. Build template configurator UI
8. Build template editor UI
9. Implement import/export
10. Write unit tests for variable resolution
11. Write E2E test for template flow

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Built-in templates available for common patterns
- [ ] Variables are configurable before instantiation
- [ ] Template creates working pipeline
- [ ] Custom templates can be created/saved
- [ ] Import/export works
- [ ] Preview shows expanded steps

## Dependencies

+{"id":"bd-u2cl8","title":"Context Window Heat Map Visualization","description":"## Overview

Visual representation of token usage over an agent's session—where tokens went (tool calls, code blocks, reasoning, system prompts). Interactive timeline helps users understand consumption patterns.

## Background & Justification

The context-health.service.ts tracks context utilization but the data is opaque. Users see "80% context used" but not where those tokens went. Visualization helps:
- Identify token-heavy operations
- Optimize prompts and workflows
- Understand context pressure points
- Debug "why did the context fill up?"

## Key Components

### 1. Token Attribution Tracking

Track token usage by category:
```typescript
interface TokenAttribution {
  timestamp: Date;
  category: 'system' | 'user' | 'assistant' | 'tool_call' | 'tool_result' | 'code' | 'reasoning';
  tokens: number;
  description?: string; // e.g., "Read file X", "Grep search"
  metadata?: Record<string, any>;
}
```

### 2. Heat Map Data Model

```typescript
interface ContextHeatMap {
  agentId: string;
  sessionId: string;
  timeRange: { start: Date; end: Date };
  buckets: HeatMapBucket[];
  totalTokens: number;
  contextLimit: number;
}

interface HeatMapBucket {
  timestamp: Date;
  duration: number; // seconds
  attributions: {
    [category: string]: number; // tokens per category
  };
  utilization: number; // 0-1
}
```

### 3. Visualization Components

**Heat Map Chart:**
- X-axis: Time (session duration)
- Y-axis: Token categories stacked
- Color intensity: Token density
- Hover: Detail tooltip

**Category Breakdown:**
- Pie/donut chart of token distribution
- Clickable segments to filter timeline

**Timeline Markers:**
- Context rotation events
- Checkpoint saves
- Tool call boundaries
- Warning thresholds

**Zoom Controls:**
- Zoom in/out on time range
- Pan across session
- Jump to events

### 4. Interactive Features

**Click on segment:**
- Show exact content (if available)
- Link to tool call or message
- Copy content option

**Filter by category:**
- Toggle categories on/off
- Compare before/after filtering

**Export:**
- PNG/SVG of heat map
- CSV of raw data
- JSON for analysis

### 5. API Endpoints

- `GET /api/agents/:id/context-heatmap` - Heat map data
- `GET /api/agents/:id/context-attributions` - Raw attributions
- `GET /api/agents/:id/context-summary` - Category totals
- WebSocket: Real-time attribution updates

## Implementation Tasks

1. Extend context tracking with attribution data
2. Create attribution aggregation logic
3. Design heat map data structure
4. Add API endpoints for heat map data
5. Build heat map chart component (use D3.js or similar)
6. Build category breakdown chart
7. Build timeline with markers
8. Implement zoom/pan controls
9. Add click-to-detail interaction
10. Implement export functionality
11. Write unit tests for aggregation
12. Write E2E test for visualization

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Heat map shows token usage over time
- [ ] Categories are visually distinct
- [ ] Hover shows detailed breakdown
- [ ] Zoom/pan works smoothly
- [ ] Click reveals source content
- [ ] Real-time updates for active agents
- [ ] Export produces usable output

## Technical Considerations

- Large sessions = many data points, need aggregation
- Use canvas for performance with many buckets
- Lazy load historical data
- WebSocket for real-time without polling

## Dependencies
